title: ROPE validation suite
description: A grid of validation cases (specific space-weather time windows) crossed with checks (error metrics and/or plots). Validate this against the whole document's top-level fields; validate each checks[i].params separately against the matching schemas/checks/<kind>.schema.json, resolved via check_kinds.json — the same two-stage pattern used for model_manifest.json (see manifest-envelope.schema.json). Whether every case in a check's resolved applies_to set actually supplies the data_refs key the check's params.truth_ref names is NOT enforced by this schema — see tests/validation_suite_test.py::test_checks_data_refs_present_on_applicable_cases (static) and the benchmarking tool that runs suites (runtime) for enforcement.

data_refs.description: Named pointers to reference/truth data files this case supplies (e.g. {"satellite_track": "..."}). Keys are matched against a check's params.truth_ref by name.

tags.description: Purely descriptive; not consumed by any check or tooling today. Lets cases be grouped/filtered later (e.g. ["storm", "quiet", "solar-max"]) without a migration pass once the suite grows past a handful of placeholder cases.

kind.description: Must appear in check_kinds.json. Not enforceable by this schema — see tests/validation_suite_test.py.

applies_to.description: List of case ids this check runs against. Absence means "all cases" — adding a new case later implicitly expands the scope of any check that omits this field.

params.description: Kind-specific parameter block, validated in a second stage against schemas/checks/<kind>.schema.json.

schema_version.description: Tracks the JSON shape of this document.

content_version.description: A separate, human-bumped counter tracking the suite's content (cases/checks), incremented whenever content changes even though schema_version stays put. Only meaningful for comparison between two suite documents sharing the same schema_version — if schema_version ever differs, content_version comparisons are undefined and should be treated as "needs re-validation", not compared numerically. This is the value a manifest's validation.suite_content_version is compared against to detect staleness.

cases.description: Placeholder case — not a real event
